reviews.review_description: I love the mixed reality part, pretty cool.

This is an outstanding Maze-style game! It combines an odd but welcome Astroids feel with a modern VR aesthetic and control scheme.  There are just a few things holding it back from being perfect!

Pro's: 
-Animations are very smooth
-Mazes are unique
-The story is fun and quirky (good voiceovers)
-Sound design is outstanding!
-Sense of scale is perfect (this is really shines as you can zoom in very close or out super far)

Con's:
-Very simplistic
-Maps feel somewhat repetitive (despite different obstacles added)
-Occasional glitch where your ship pops outside of the maze
-Game menus have some overlapping issues
-Difficulty ramps up quicker than expected
-Controls are difficult to wrap your head around and master (I'm still trying to get used to the controls)

Overall, this game is a blast to play, and I found myself fully enjoying it, to the point where I took the headset off and realized I just burned through 2 hours and nearly a full battery. There will be some areas you get stuck on, but finally getting through them is one of the best feelings. 

For the price, this game is a good one!

Do you know the game "hot-wire" where you have to avoid touching the wire? This game is basically that, but it is much more fun and has a story.

You have to fly a spaceship through mazes, but you see the game in third person and your controller is a remote control motion controller. The controls feel really good. (I didn't know I could twist my hand like that).

The levels are cool and they have challenging combat. It's definitely not easy, but that's good. They are all pretty similar though.

The story is not bad and is told through dialog, but with full voice acting and that is pretty nice.

But not everything is perfect. The graphics are simple and the skybox looks boring after a while. Same with the soundtrack, it's annoying after a while.

All in all it's a nice game and I would definitely recommend it to people who are fans of sit-arcade style games in VR.
The space environment is really cool with the music and giving a dimensional vr mechanic with very good voice actors. The character art is very cool!

I am not a core fantasy gamer but I could be if there were more games like this.  It is a different experience so far on my journey with Quest 2 and once I got used to the controls I was able to navigate through quickly and loved having views in the maze.  I felt more immersed in the game vs other apps.  Being able to use both left and right hand controls gave me more dimension gameplay, enjoyed the quirky characters but they got a bit annoying on repeat when you failed the task. Be good to be able to rotate the music and characters comments in future revs so a beginner like me won’t get so annoyed/frustrated.  Multi-player would be a bonus. Recommended for a wide variety of VR enthusiasts.

I recently played "Shuttle Maze - VR Maze & Action Hybrid" and I must say, I was thoroughly impressed by the game. Ayayu Games has done a fantastic job of incorporating VR features into a classic 2D platform game, making for an enjoyable and immersive experience.

As a fan of classic games and maze puzzles, I was immediately drawn to "Shuttle Maze". The objective of helping Glow escape from the bizarre space station ruled by a mad AI, and the added excitement of blasting and shooting enemy bots made for entertaining and engaging gameplay.

The use of Dr. Harris's upgraded weapons added an extra layer of excitement to the game and the retro graphics and vibrant beats only enhanced the overall experience. I was also impressed with the motion sickness-free gameplay, allowing for extended periods of play without any discomfort.

The ability to control the spaceship in all 6 directions while moving freely around the large mazes was a fantastic touch. The challenge of finding Dr. Harris added an extra layer of depth to the game, making it more than just a simple in-and-out mission.

Overall, I highly recommend "Shuttle Maze - VR Maze & Action Hybrid" to anyone who loves classic games and solving maze puzzles. The VR evolution of this classic genre is a treat to play and Ayayu Games should be proud of their fantastic creation.
grate game, thanks

Fun game with well made animations and mechanics.
The camera controls could use a bit more work.
Recommend
very unique gme

I'm an avid Quest 2 gamer and have played a lot of different games from many different genres. Shuttlemaze was a pleasant surprise - immersive arcade nostalgia at it's finest. The gameplay is unique - easy to grasp but challenging to master; the visuals are immersive - they put you into the world while still maintaining the proper arcade distance; and the music is awesome - the soundtrack has an 80s synth vibe that I enjoyed as much as the game itself. If you're looking for a fun game that is both challenging and relaxing, I highly recommend Shuttlemaze.

I really like SHUTTLE MAZE. It has a lot to offer, and is interesting in many ways. First, it has a nice retro style brilliant graphics. There are elements of Jump'n Run, Adventure, Puzzles, Racing, and it requires patience and skills.

Controls are special, so one really has to train oneself to get better and accurate in moving the little space ship. I particularly love the way one can zoom in and turn the view with the left controller. 

Thanks to the developers for this rather new experience in VR 3D. They made perfect use of the three-dimensional options in VR gaming 👌

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for the great review! Deeply appreciated :)

reviews.review_description: well i have played a lot games. this one is one of those games where i spent hours together, and the story line is awesome. well suggesting to all my network about this one. waiting for more and more upgrade.

First off, I got this game for free from the developer. But that has no bearing on the score. The game itself is addictive, I tend to play it in little bursts as I do with most games. Just like other's have said the voice acting is great (: It really helps immerse you into the story. 

I haven't completed it by any stretch but from what I have played it's been great :) 

As with most games on the VR, I really wish there was a multiplayer or co-op aspect so I can play with my best mate. But keep up the good work!
Imagine you can control a little spaceship in a maze made of tubes.. Infested by robots.... Full of keys and doors. Great idea.... I'm enjoying it a lot! Un saluto alla grande community VR ITALIA!

Great game!
Positives: 
1. A very different gameplay where you try to get out the maze by controlling a spaceship in third person. 
2. Different game mechanics for a change, since most of the vr games these days are first person.
3. Controlling of ship is difficult initially but is very intuitive and in a few minutes of playing, You'll start cruising around at high speeds, blasting the doors in your way.
4. comic book like artwork for story/characters 
Negatives: 
1. While some may find this third person experience new and interesting, others may still prefer traditional first person game, depending on individual preference.
2. Surrounding/environment is static dark which somewhat limits the immersion, would be interesting to see dynamic/interactive environments as well

Hope you find this review somewhat useful.
Cheers guys! Have a great day

Full disclosure: I played this game with a code provided by the developer. Further, I only played for maybe 30 minutes.

I did enjoy the time I spent playing this one, but I’m not so sure I would have paid money for it, probably because games like this are not my cup of tea. Still, it must be a great game (for the majority of Quest gamers), given all the glowing reviews here, so I’ll just assume I’m the problem here. Rather than hurt the overall score here, I’ll leave 4 stars. Maybe I’ll give it another try, but I have so many other things I prefer doing instead at the moment.

reviews.review_title: Not bad, not yet the best
reviews.review_description:
ShuttleMaze is not your ordinary VR game,
It is a semi-space simulation

Since the first moment you play you get a story, a fun plot that is not too original but may surpass the average netflix series.
I found it fun to imagine this Sci-Fi life in space and the characters really spice it up.

At first the movement feels a bit shaky, you'll get used to it, you don't have to move perfectly, just to get into the movement flow.
I found myself sitting pretty fast because the camera movement is well designed.

The only problem is that I encountered a bug when changing to stationary boundary, the enemies stopped moving and I could not continue the level, a quick restart does the trick.

I find it hard to call it a maze game because beyond searching for a route there is no game mechanic to make the maze part of the game more interesting or challenging.
What we do have here is combat, and boy its one hell of a combat system.
Whether by changing weapons, being able to shoot towards any direction or planning how many enemies to take on by repositioning.

You could picture a similar game on other consoles, the VR experience here is mainly fun when you aim your ships cannons, one thing I found fun is looking back after a few minutes and being able to see all the rooms you already passed through.

So if you want a quick game for your down time, to relax and have fun, with a good story and jolly movement, this is the game for you!
Get this game your your library if like maze. It’s fun and a good story.

This is the perfect game to just chill and have a TRON-esque experience just with a different perspective. The gameplay mechanics, animation, art direction, VFX were all really great. The story is the not the highlight of this title but it is quite fun to play nevertheless.

When the game first loads it gives you that first steps graphics feeling that I initially had on my first use of the quest2. It’s a completely different feeling, you can zoom in and out, and rotate 360 degrees around a mini space ship stuck inside a maze. Think of a hamster in a hamster tube but a spaceship with outstanding graphics and attention to detail. 

Takes awhile to get to grips of the controls but it’s a game I’ll keep coming back to.
